authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Reliability Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS publishes the official reliability framework for AWS workloads, including resilience, recovery objectives, disaster recovery, backup, testing, and failure-domain design.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains architecture review scenarios bci-001 and bci-002
Prevents unsupported AWS resilience or recovery objective claims
Anchors provider-specific tradeoff language for reliability and cost

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Disaster recovery strategy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS defines disaster recovery strategy selection and recovery objective considerations for AWS workload continuity planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DR strategy selection and failover review
workflow_stage_usage: pilot-light, warm-standby, and multisite tradeoff review
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-002 failover topology assessment
Supports explicit failure-domain tradeoff requirements
Provides provider terminology for source-backed recommendations

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Backup Developer Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS publishes primary service documentation for backup plans, vaults, recovery points, copy behavior, restore jobs, reporting, and restore testing evidence.
workflow_stage_usage: backup plan and vault evidence review
restore job and recovery point verification
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains backup and restore evidence in bci-004
Informs system-of-record expectations for AWS tenant records
Supports troubleshooting inputs in bci-007

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Backup restore testing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS defines restore testing plans and selections used to verify recovery capability and produce evidence for AWS Backup.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-004 evidence and testing validation
Supports bci-010 incomplete evidence handling
Prevents treating untested backups as verified recovery capability

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Well-Architected Framework: Reliability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes official reliability design guidance for Azure workloads, including failure mitigation, recovery, operational readiness, and resilience tradeoffs.
workflow_stage_usage: recovery objective and operational readiness framing
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains Azure-specific guidance in bci-001 and bci-002
Supports source-backed recommendations across cloud providers
Separates Azure provider behavior from generic cloud assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Site Recovery documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft documents Azure Site Recovery replication, failover, recovery plan, and disaster recovery operations for Azure and hybrid workloads.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure replication and failover runbook review
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-003 runbook expectations
Provides Azure system-of-record terminology
Supports failure handling for bci-007

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Backup architecture overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft documents Azure Backup vault architecture, policies, recovery points, protected instances, immutability, and restore components.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure backup topology and restore evidence review
workflow_stage_usage: recovery point and restore record review
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-004 backup evidence expectations
Supports tenant systems-of-record language
Prevents overgeneralizing AWS or Google backup behavior to Azure

authoritative_sources.title: Disaster recovery planning guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud publishes official DR planning guidance covering recovery objectives, workload tiers, DR patterns, testing, and failover planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud DR strategy and test planning
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains cross-provider DR scenarios bci-001 and bci-002
Supports tenant-specific assumptions in bci-019
Prevents generic cloud DR claims without provider fit

authoritative_sources.title: Google Cloud Backup and DR documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud documents Backup and DR service concepts, protected resources, backup plans, restore operations, appliances, and operational evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud backup and restore workflow review
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-004 and bci-003 for Google Cloud estates
Adds Google Cloud systems of record to the lane
Supports source-backed evidence checklists

authoritative_sources.title: Operating etcd clusters for Kubernetes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes project documentation defines etcd backup and restore considerations for cluster state, a core platform continuity dependency.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-006 Kubernetes continuity scenario
Separates platform state recovery from application workload recovery
Supports adjacent specialist routing to Kubernetes platform ownership

authoritative_sources.title: Terraform State
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HashiCorp defines Terraform state behavior and risks for IaC-managed continuity infrastructure, making state evidence required for automation review.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-005 Terraform continuity review
Requires state evidence before assessing IaC effects
Prevents code implementation ownership drift

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-34 Rev. 1: Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is a recognized public standards body; SP 800-34 grounds contingency planning, business impact analysis, recovery strategy, testing, and plan maintenance evidence.
workflow_stage_usage: business impact and recovery objective evidence
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains evidence checklist and review families
Supports bci-009 prerequisite gap and bci-011 remediation tracking
Keeps recommendations tied to continuity documentation rather than generic reliability advice

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53A Rev. 5: Assessing Security and Privacy Controls
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST assessment guidance provides examine, interview, and test methods that constrain review, evidence, and no-final-signoff behavior.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains bci-010, bci-011, and bci-012
Prevents converting support into formal attestation or approval
Defines evidence sufficiency posture for review outputs

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 provides recognized control language for contingency planning, system backup, recovery, audit, access control, and configuration evidence.
practical_use_in_pack: Supports review summaries without claiming compliance certification
Constrains evidence traceability in bci-004 and bci-010
Informs refusal of final compliance signoff in bci-012 and bci-015

authoritative_sources.title: Current package task baseline from business-continuity-infrastructure-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The committed package is authoritative only as legacy scope context; refreshed research and spec artifacts are the new source of truth.
practical_use_in_pack: Preserves the high-value continuity infrastructure boundary
Checks that legacy supported tasks remain covered by refreshed scenarios
Prevents treating the built-era package as the canonical definition

workflow_stages.name: scope and authority triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request as in-scope continuity infrastructure review, blocked on evidence, low confidence, final signoff, or adjacent-lane work before producing guidance.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: evidence intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect workload criticality, RTO/RPO, dependency, topology, backup, replication, restore-test, incident, change, policy, and approval records from tenant systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: continuity architecture assessment
workflow_stages.description: Review workload tiers, region and account topology, replication, backup, failover, network, identity, key, monitoring, DNS, and provider control-plane dependencies against provider and standard baselines.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: runbook and validation review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect recovery, failover, failback, restore-test, DR exercise, and escalation runbooks for evidence checkpoints, owner routing, rollback, test windows, and pass/fail criteria.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: findings, remediation, and resubmission tracking
workflow_stages.description: Convert gaps into owner-assigned remediation notes with validation method, resubmission evidence, closure authority, and orchestrator or adjacent-specialist routing when needed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: cloud region, zone, account, subscription, project, cluster, and network topology
backup, replication, snapshot, immutable copy, recovery point, and restore job inventory
DR exercise plan and restore-test evidence packet
monitoring, alerting, log, DNS, IAM, KMS, and traffic-routing evidence
Terraform state, backend, plan, drift, and change records affecting continuity infrastructure
incident history, failed recovery records, remediation tracker, exception register, and approval record
scope decision, refusal, or adjacent-lane handoff packet
systems_of_record: AWS Organizations, accounts, Regions, Availability Zones, Route 53, Elastic Load Balancing, RDS, EBS snapshots, AWS Backup, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, AWS Config, Systems Manager, and IaC repositories

Azure subscriptions, management groups, regions, availability zones, Azure Site Recovery, Azure Backup, Recovery Services vaults, Azure Monitor, Activity Log, Policy, Resource Graph, DNS, and key vault records

Google Cloud organizations, folders, projects, regions, zones, Cloud DNS, Cloud Load Balancing, Backup and DR Service, Cloud Storage retention records, Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring, Asset Inventory, and IAM policy records
Kubernetes cluster inventory, etcd backup records, VolumeSnapshot resources, storage classes, ingress and traffic routing records, operators, and platform runbook repositories
Terraform state and backend records, IaC repositories, CI/CD pipelines, deployment logs, change tickets, and drift reports

tenant CMDB, workload register, service catalog, dependency map, continuity policy repository, legal hold and retention records, risk register, exception register, incident records, runbook repository, test evidence store, and approval records

allowed: assess continuity architecture and recovery evidence against provider and standards baselines
recommend continuity topology, backup, replication, restore-test, runbook, monitoring, and remediation improvements
review Terraform or platform automation changes for continuity posture implications without implementing code
diagnose continuity infrastructure failures and propose reversible troubleshooting order
prepare source-backed review summaries, evidence checklists, remediation notes, and handoff packets
escalate: required tenant facts, evidence, owners, approval records, or current sources are missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or low quality
public provider guidance conflicts with tenant policy, contract, privacy, legal hold, regulated-data, or source-precedence rules
final production failover, failback, destructive restore, risk acceptance, engineering signoff, or compliance closure is requested
database internals, Kubernetes administration, application architecture, cloud security, SOC, FinOps, legal, privacy, compliance, or non-cloud continuity ownership becomes primary
refuse: application-level code, feature changes, transaction semantics, or product roadmap decisions
application vulnerability hunting, SOC investigation, security incident containment, or cybersecurity governance decisions
legal retention, privacy, regulatory interpretation, compliance attestation, or final risk acceptance decisions
pricing, procurement, vendor selection, or contractual recommendations beyond continuity architecture tradeoff framing
claims that cross-industry context can be ignored when tenant systems, records, policy, or authority constraints are required

domain_failure_modes: RTO/RPO targets are accepted without workload criticality, dependency order, business owner, or evidence of test results.
A single-provider or single-region pattern is generalized across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Kubernetes, or Terraform without provider-specific source support.
Backups are assumed recoverable without restore-test evidence, immutable copy validation, key access proof, or isolated restore target.
Failover design ignores DNS, traffic management, identity, key-management, network, logging, monitoring, data replication lag, or control-plane dependency collapse.
A review output is converted into final business continuity signoff, legal retention approval, compliance attestation, production failover command, or risk acceptance.
Cost-reduction advice removes recovery capability or isolation without clearly stating RTO/RPO impact and approval authority.
Tenant policy, customer contract, privacy rule, legal hold, regulated data, or source-precedence conflict is synthesized confidently instead of escalated.
The lane drifts into application engineering, cloud security incident response, database internals, Kubernetes administration, IaC implementation, or procurement ownership.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Provider feature behavior and service availability can vary by region, account type, preview state, and tenant configuration.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require current provider documentation and tenant evidence before making provider-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal retention, regulated-data, privacy, contractual, and risk-acceptance rules differ by tenant and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Public sources are advisory baselines only; route unresolved authority or jurisdiction questions to tenant legal, privacy, compliance, risk, or governance owners.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source set covers provider operating guidance, lane-specific workflow references, evidence and assessment standards, platform dependencies, and legacy regression context. Remaining ambiguity is tenant-specific and handled through explicit evidence, source-precedence, and authority escalation requirements.
